LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID CLIFTON'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED UNDER CCP 473(A)(1). DEFENDANT CLIFTON HAS PROVIDED EVIDENCE THAT THE PROPOSED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS COMPULSORY UNDER CCP 426.30, AS IT ARISES FROM THE SAME TRANSACTION OR OCCURRENCE AS THE ALLEGATIONS IN PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT. FURTHERMORE, PLAINTIFF HAS PROVIDED EVIDENCE THAT THE DELAY IN FILING THE COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS NOT IN BAD FAITH AS HE WAS INCARCERATED WHEN THE ORIGINAL COMPLAINT WAS FILED, AND PRISONER LEGAL SERVICES WAS LIMITED IN ASSISTING MR. CLIFTON IN THIS MATTER. (SEE DECLARATION OF SULLIVAN IN SUPPORT OF DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO FILE A CROSS-COMPLAINT, 2, EXH. B.) ANY CHALLENGE TO THE SUFFICIENCY OR MERITS OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT SHOULD BE ADDRESSED AFTER IT IS FILED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-09-2013 Continued To Mar-24-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Leave To File X-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT DAVID CLIFTON's Motion For Leave To File cross-Complaint is granted under CCP 473(a)(1). Defendant Clifton has provided evidence that the proposed cross-complaint is compulsory under CCP 426.30, as it arises from the same transaction or occurrence as the allegations in Plaintiffs' complaint. Furthermore, Plaintiff has provided evidence that the delay in filing the compulsory cross-complaint was not in bad faith as he was incarcerated when the original complaint was filed, and Prisoner Legal Services was limited in assisting Mr. Clifton in this matter. (See Declaration of Sullivan in Support of Defendant's Motion to File a Cross-Complaint, 2, Exh. B.) Any challenge to the sufficiency or merits of the cross-complaint should be addressed after it is filed. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J.
